skills.description: Run evidence-first QA with actor/evaluator separation and reproducible findings. Use when Codex should execute bounded scenarios, capture artifacts, or produce QA verdicts.
skills.genericInstruction: Run the smallest repository-owned QA scenario and keep captured observations separate from evaluator judgment.

skills.description: Reproduce and shape evidence into a bounded next lane. Use when Codex should strengthen a finding, check prior coverage, or prepare an issue-ready packet.
skills.genericInstruction: Strengthen the evidence before shaping work, and keep low-confidence findings report-only.

skills.description: Prepare evidence-grounded governance recommendations for explicit human gates. Use when Codex encounters a named launch, privacy, legal, trust, or external-side-effect boundary.
skills.genericInstruction: Start only for an explicit repository-owned governance boundary and return a recommendation to the named human decision owner.

skills.description: Evaluate product coherence against a goal harness and route the smallest evidence-backed gap. Use when Codex should score behavior, shape work, or choose the next specialist lane.
skills.genericInstruction: Use the repository's goal harness and routing vocabulary; do not infer implementation authority from an evaluation request.

skills.description: Execute one accepted issue as one bounded reviewable change. Use when Codex has decision-complete scope, repository-granted write authority, and a human-reviewed delivery lane.
skills.genericInstruction: Execute exactly one accepted scope, preserve unrelated state, validate proportionally to risk, and leave approval to the repository's human gate.

skills.description: Turn ambiguous or cross-cutting work into a decision-complete plan. Use when Codex should research options, grill assumptions, classify risk, or route work before implementation.
skills.genericInstruction: Resolve planning ambiguity with evidence and escalate unresolved policy or human judgment instead of implementing through it.
